query: VHS scanlines TV static analog distortion CRT screen
message: sub:analog_degradation/VHS_CRT — anti_aesthetic — scanlines, TV static, color bars, pixel grids, retro CRT

response.objective_caption: A close-up view of a repeating geometric zigzag pattern filling the frame. The composition features interlocking angular shapes in deep blue and teal, accented by bright green forms and small red-orange details. Closely spaced horizontal lines create a distinct ridged texture across the entire surface. The chevrons are arranged in diagonal rows, producing a rhythmic, undulating visual movement.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A close-up of a zigzag chevron pattern in blue, teal, green, and red-orange subjected to deliberate analog degradation and abstract photographic techniques. Heavy VHS-style scanlines and tracking errors create dense horizontal lines across the entire frame, evoking aged analog media. The non-representational treatment dissolves the subject into pure geometric texture and color, foregrounding the retro lo-fi aesthetic and the material imperfection of damaged video signals.
